headline: Trump Says They Discussed Chips, China Will Talk To NVIDIA And Others About Taking Chips, But Blackwell Chips Were Not Discussed
Elon Musk's $1 Trillion Tesla Pay Plan Would 'Concentrate Power,' Says Pension Plan Holding Over $2 Billion In TSLA Stock
summary: CalPERS reportedly planning to vote against Musk's $1 trillion compensation award due to concerns of power consolidation, among others.

headline: Sam Altman's OpenAI Targets Up To $1 Trillion Valuation In Historic IPO After Major Restructuring And California Approval: Report
summary: OpenAI is reportedly preparing for a potential IPO that could value the ChatGPT maker at up to $1 trillion as early as 2026–2027, following a major restructuring, regulatory approval in California, and renewed confidence from key investor Microsoft.

headline: Meta, Alphabet, Chipotle, Microsoft And Starbucks: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
summary: Major stock indexes traded mixed on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipping nearly 0.2% to 47,632 and the S&P 500 holding steady at 6,890.59.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg Is A 'Wartime CEO' And Leading Meta Into Next Stage Of AI Revolution, Says Dan Ives — Stock Sell-Off Is Short-Sighted
summary: Wedbush's Dan Ives called Meta's post-earnings stock plunge "short-sighted," saying CEO Mark Zuckerberg's massive spending on AI infrastructure proves he's a "wartime CEO" steering Meta into the next phase of the AI revolution.

headline: Steve Jobs Once Stressed The Importance Of 'Spontaneous Meetings' — Now Jamie Dimon Warns 'Young People Are Being Left Behind' For The Same Reason
summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon echoed Steve Jobs' belief that creativity and mentorship thrive through in-person collaboration, warning that remote work is leaving young employees "behind" as tech giants like Apple, Google, and Tesla also push back against work-from-home culture.
